verses.text: The princes hear Jeremiah urge surrender to live, and resolve he must die. His counsel enrages them. To tell a doomed people the only way of life can provoke their fury.
The princes hear Jeremiah urge surrender to live, and resolve he must die. His counsel enrages them. To tell a doomed people the only way of life can provoke their fury.
The princes hear Jeremiah urge surrender to live, and resolve he must die. His counsel enrages them. To tell a doomed people the only way of life can provoke their fury.
They cast him into a miry dungeon, where he 'sunk in the mire.' He is left to perish. The faithful messenger is treated as a malefactor.
They cast him into a miry dungeon, where he 'sunk in the mire.' He is left to perish. The faithful messenger is treated as a malefactor.
They cast him into a miry dungeon, where he 'sunk in the mire.' He is left to perish. The faithful messenger is treated as a malefactor.
Ebed-melech the Ethiopian boldly tells the king the princes have done evil, and Jeremiah will die of hunger. A foreigner shows courage. God raises up unexpected friends for His servants.
Ebed-melech the Ethiopian boldly tells the king the princes have done evil, and Jeremiah will die of hunger. A foreigner shows courage. God raises up unexpected friends for His servants.
Ebed-melech the Ethiopian boldly tells the king the princes have done evil, and Jeremiah will die of hunger. A foreigner shows courage. God raises up unexpected friends for His servants.
The king commands his rescue; with cords and old rags for padding they draw Jeremiah up. He is gently lifted. God turns the heart of the king to deliver His prophet.
The king commands his rescue; with cords and old rags for padding they draw Jeremiah up. He is gently lifted. God turns the heart of the king to deliver His prophet.
The king commands his rescue; with cords and old rags for padding they draw Jeremiah up. He is gently lifted. God turns the heart of the king to deliver His prophet.
The king commands his rescue; with cords and old rags for padding they draw Jeremiah up. He is gently lifted. God turns the heart of the king to deliver His prophet.
Zedekiah secretly asks counsel; fearing for his life, Jeremiah is reassured by the king's oath. He speaks under protection. The prophet weighs his danger yet prepares to tell the truth.
Zedekiah secretly asks counsel; fearing for his life, Jeremiah is reassured by the king's oath. He speaks under protection. The prophet weighs his danger yet prepares to tell the truth.
Zedekiah secretly asks counsel; fearing for his life, Jeremiah is reassured by the king's oath. He speaks under protection. The prophet weighs his danger yet prepares to tell the truth.

He urges the king, 'if thou wilt... go forth unto the king of Babylon's princes, then thy soul shall live'; obey the LORD. Surrender is the way of life. God's word offers escape if the king will trust it.

He urges the king, 'if thou wilt... go forth unto the king of Babylon's princes, then thy soul shall live'; obey the LORD. Surrender is the way of life. God's word offers escape if the king will trust it.
But if he refuses, his women and children shall be taken, and he shall cause the city to be burned. The warning is grave. To disobey God's word brings ruin on oneself and others.
But if he refuses, his women and children shall be taken, and he shall cause the city to be burned. The warning is grave. To disobey God's word brings ruin on oneself and others.
But if he refuses, his women and children shall be taken, and he shall cause the city to be burned. The warning is grave. To disobey God's word brings ruin on oneself and others.
Zedekiah binds Jeremiah to secrecy, and the prophet remains in the prison court till the city falls. He keeps the king's confidence. The faithful servant is wise as well as bold.
